teachings.teaching: In times of deep distress (“agony”), one should pray more earnestly rather than withdrawing.
Genuine prayer can be marked by intense sincerity and spiritual struggle, not just casual words.
Suffering may be profoundly real and can be accompanied by visible signs, emphasizing the seriousness of the moment.
Facing trials with prayer demonstrates trust and commitment to God during anguish.

goals.goal: To portray the intensity of Jesus’ anguish and to highlight that even in extreme suffering he responds with intensified, earnest prayer.
